why_matters: DesktopHostSlotMap type has a TypeScript indexing issue — SlotName generic can't index the map type because Component<{}> doesn't satisfy Record<string, any> constraint. This blocks clean typecheck for all packages that depend on @opencode-ai/plugin.
best_practice_anchor: TypeScript generic constraints with mapped types
recommended_slice: Fix DesktopHostSlotMap to use a proper indexed access type pattern — either use a type alias for each slot's expected props or use `Record<SlotName, Component<{}>>` directly instead of a mapped type over keyof Slots

why_matters: record-execution-wave.ts imports from '../storage/duckdb-pipe' but no such module exists. This causes a runtime crash when the tool is loaded and blocks bun test for any test importing this tool.
best_practice_anchor: Module resolution — import must match existing file
recommended_slice: Create the missing duckdb-pipe module or fix the import in record-execution-wave.ts to point to the correct module

why_matters: installCli IPC channel is exposed via contextBridge in preload but no ipcMain.handle exists — any renderer call will hang indefinitely. This is a blocking bug in the desktops security/augmentation boundary.
best_practice_anchor: IPC handler registration must be 1:1 with preload bridge exposure — every preload API must have a corresponding handler registered before app ready.
recommended_slice: Audit all 42 IPC channels for orphaned preload→handler mappings. Implement installCli handler or remove the channel. Add a CI check that verifies preload channels have corresponding ipcMain handles.

why_matters: Pre-existing typecheck errors in @opencode-ai/app package: Property 'setOnboarded' and 'showSessionViz' do not exist on settings type. These indicate settings type definition drift — the context's type interface is out of sync with the consuming components. This blocks clean typecheck in the desktop package.
best_practice_anchor: TypeScript strict mode — settings type should be generated from a single source of truth
recommended_slice: Audit the Settings context type definition in @opencode-ai/app, add missing properties (setOnboarded, showSessionViz, setShowSessionViz), and fix all consumer components to match.

why_matters: The handoff briefing has a JSON syntax error on line 193 (literal array values written without brackets: 'lines: 159, 559, 562, 565, 568' should be 'lines: [159, 559, 562, 565, 568]'). This breaks read_artifact's JSON parser. The next session won't be able to read the briefing programmatically.
best_practice_anchor: JSON RFC 8259 — array values must be enclosed in brackets
recommended_slice: Fix the JSON syntax and add a validation check to the handoff generation script that validates JSON before writing.

why_matters: ipc.ts imports './install-cli' which does not exist, causing type check failures (TS2307). The install-cli handler was added but the module was never created. This blocks clean type checking of the desktop package.
best_practice_anchor: Prevent unreachable module imports — every import must resolve to a file at type check time
recommended_slice: Either create install-cli.ts with the installCli function stub, or remove the install-cli handler from ipc.ts if the feature was abandoned

why_matters: The file dialog-settings.tsx imports ./settings-agents which does not exist. This causes TS2307 and blocks the desktop typecheck from passing with 0 errors, since tsgo -b builds the full project graph.
best_practice_anchor: All imports must resolve to existing modules
recommended_slice: Create packages/app/src/components/settings-agents.tsx or remove the import from dialog-settings.tsx

why_matters: Bare integer values at line 193 (559, 562, 565, 568) instead of array brackets cause JSON parse failure — downstream consumers cannot read the handoff artifact
best_practice_anchor: JSON RFC 8259 — arrays must be enclosed in square brackets
recommended_slice: Fix JSON syntax in next-session-briefing.v1.json: wrap line 193 values in [] brackets

why_matters: Prepublication review records listing a reviewer with a positive verdict before that reviewer has conducted their review undermines the entire governance process. Combined with untracked (non-committed) evidence artifacts, this creates a system where governance can be retroactively altered with no git traceability. Any future session relying on these records cannot verify their authenticity against committed history.
best_practice_anchor: AGENTS.md — Prepublication Review Loop: 'The reviewer report is append-only and may not be edited or rewritten by the builder.' A record that pre-awards a reviewer verdict is structurally equivalent to rewriting the reviewer report before the review occurs.
recommended_slice: Governance artifact immutability: Implement a schema constraint that prevents prepublication_verdict records from being written with 'admitted' verdict until the reviewer tool records it. Add a git pre-commit or pre-push hook requiring governance artifacts to be committed alongside the published SHA.

why_matters: The plan references window.electronAPI in Step 4 (e.g., ``window.electronAPI.getMcpServers()``) but the convention across all existing desktop IPC usage is window.api. The contextBridge exposes as contextBridge.exposeInMainWorld("api", api) (packages/desktop/src/preload/index.ts:89), and all consumers — dialog-manage-agents.tsx:17, app.tsx:71-80 — access IPC methods via window.api.*, not window.electronAPI.*. The ElectronAPI type is just the TypeScript interface name, not the runtime key. Using the wrong runtime key would result in undefined method calls at runtime.
best_practice_anchor: Codebase convention: IPC bridge key is "api" not "electronAPI"
recommended_slice: Replace all `window.electronAPI` references in the plan with `window.api` to match the existing convention.

why_matters: The plan's dialog-edit-mcp code sketch uses multiple createSignal calls for form state (name, type, command, url, etc.). The app package's AGENTS.md explicitly states "Always prefer createStore over multiple createSignal calls". The existing dialog-edit-project.tsx follows this convention with createStore for its multi-field form, while dialog-edit-agent.tsx predates this rule and still uses createSignal. A new component should follow the current convention, not the legacy one.
best_practice_anchor: packages/app/AGENTS.md: "Always prefer createStore over multiple createSignal calls"
recommended_slice: Update the plan's dialog-edit-mcp.tsx to use createStore for form state instead of multiple createSignal calls.

why_matters: The plan's code sketch for dialog-edit-mcp uses raw string literals for dialog titles ("Edit MCP Server" / "Add MCP Server") and form labels. The established dialog-edit-agent.tsx pattern uses language.t() for all user-facing strings and the plan already adds the corresponding i18n keys in Step 5. Using raw strings instead of language.t() creates a gap between the i18n additions and the actual UI, and diverges from the component pattern the plan claims to follow.
best_practice_anchor: Codebase convention: all user-facing strings use language.t()
recommended_slice: Update the dialog-edit-mcp code sketch to use language.t() for all user-facing strings, referencing the i18n keys defined in Step 5.

why_matters: The plan's code sketch for dialog-edit-mcp omits the standard action footer with cancel/save buttons entirely. The dialog-edit-agent.tsx pattern it claims to follow has a consistent footer structure: two Button components in a flex-end row (ghost cancel + primary save with disabled when name is empty). Omitting this from the plan means an implementer has to reconstruct the pattern from scratch, increasing the chance of inconsistency.
best_practice_anchor: Codebase convention: dialog-edit-agent.tsx footer pattern
recommended_slice: Add the standard cancel/save button footer to the dialog-edit-mcp code sketch, following the same structure as dialog-edit-agent.tsx.

why_matters: Step 4 imports `ConfirmDialog` from `@opencode-ai/ui/confirm-dialog`, but this UI component does not exist anywhere in the codebase (confirmed by ripgrep returning zero matches). All confirmation patterns in the codebase use inline state (e.g., dialog-manage-agents.tsx uses a confirmDelete signal with inline Show/Button elements). Building against a nonexistent import would fail at typecheck time.
best_practice_anchor: Codebase convention: inline confirmation UI, no dedicated confirm-dialog component
recommended_slice: Replace `ConfirmDialog` import with the inline confirmation pattern used in dialog-manage-agents.tsx (confirmDelete signal + Show/Button elements).

why_matters: The plan's inline McpServerEntry type definition makes command and url optional (command?: string[], url?: string). However, the actual SDK generated types (packages/sdk/js/src/v2/gen/types.gen.ts:1131-1144, 1155-1173) define McpLocalConfig.command as required (command: Array<string>) and McpRemoteConfig.url as required (url: string). The conditional requirement should be expressed as a discriminated union (McpLocalConfig | McpRemoteConfig), not optional fields. Using the wrong type definition would cause typecheck errors and potentially allow saving invalid data to electron-store.
best_practice_anchor: SDK generated types authority: discriminated union for MCP config
recommended_slice: Replace the plan's inline McpServerEntry type with a discriminated union using the actual SDK types (McpLocalConfig | McpRemoteConfig) wrapped with a name field, as shown in the plan's Step 6 types/mcp.ts file (which already does this correctly).

why_matters: Step 4 proposes a createEffect pattern for loading electron-store MCP entries on mount: `createEffect(() => { if (!sync.isReady || !sync.data.mcp) return; void syncMcpWithStore() })`. The established pattern in dialog-manage-agents.tsx:35-50 uses imperative loading with an `if (!loaded())` guard and direct promise chaining, not SolidJS effects. Using createEffect for one-shot data loading is an anti-pattern — effects fire on dependency changes, not just mount, which could cause duplicate injection on every sync.data.mcp change.
best_practice_anchor: Codebase convention: imperative state loading with loaded() guard per dialog-manage-agents.tsx
recommended_slice: Replace createEffect with the imperative loaded() guard pattern used in dialog-manage-agents.tsx.

why_matters: OAuth clientSecret is stored as plaintext in electron-store. Any field matching /secret|token|password|key/i should use safeStorage.encryptString() for enterprise readiness. Currently acceptable for local-only desktop use.
best_practice_anchor: Electron safeStorage API
recommended_slice: Add safeStorage encryption to electron-store for sensitive fields across all store namespaces (custom agents and MCP servers)

why_matters: MCP commands are stored as string arrays but displayed and edited as space-joined strings (join on display, split on whitespace for save). This breaks for commands with quoted arguments like `/bin/sh -c "echo hi"` or paths with spaces.
best_practice_anchor: shell-quote npm package for round-trip shell argument parsing
recommended_slice: Add shell-quote or similar library to handle proper shell argument parsing when editing MCP local server commands

why_matters: Zero test coverage for the @mention system, slash commands, and composer agent controls — any delegation feature added without tests has no regression safety net.
best_practice_anchor: Testing convention: Add component tests for handleAtSelect, handleSlashSelect, addPart, createPill, parseFromDOM, and PromptPopover rendering
recommended_slice: Add unit tests for prompt-input @mention pipeline: AtOption filtering, handleAtSelect, createPill, parseFromDOM, PromptPopover rendering

why_matters: review-tab.tsx line 159 has a type error: '"sm"' is not assignable to '"large" | "normal" | "small"'. This blocks typecheck from passing cleanly across the whole package.
best_practice_anchor: TypeScript strict type check
recommended_slice: Fix the 'sm' → 'small' type annotation in review-tab.tsx to match the allowed union

why_matters: NamedError 'Failed' is defined (line 76) but never used. Dead code from a previous design that's now misleading. Meanwhile validation errors in startAuth/authenticate/finishAuth use raw throw which bypasses Effect's typed error channel, making them uncatchable via Effect.catch.
best_practice_anchor: Effect typed errors should be the only error channel. Dead exports should be removed.
recommended_slice: Clean up dead NamedError, convert throw statements in startAuth/authenticate/finishAuth to Effect.fail with typed errors.

why_matters: isValidAgentDef (line 58) only checks id/name/prompt as string types. temperature/top_p are silently accepted as any type including strings, NaN, or Infinity — corrupted data persists through validation to electron-store. This undermines all UI-side validation.
best_practice_anchor: Defense in depth: server-side validation must cover all numeric fields that the UI presents as editable
recommended_slice: Strengthen isValidAgentDef to validate obj.temperature === undefined || typeof obj.temperature === 'number' && obj.temperature >= 0 && obj.temperature <= 2, same for top_p

why_matters: githubClearToken removes the access token from electron-store but never calls GitHub's token revocation endpoint (DELETE /applications/{client_id}/token). Orphaned tokens remain valid until expiry or manual revocation.
best_practice_anchor: OAuth token lifecycle best practice
recommended_slice: Implement token revocation: after clearing the encrypted token from store, POST to https://api.github.com/applications/{client_id}/token with Basic auth (client_id:client_secret) to revoke the token server-side.

why_matters: pendingOAuth Map (ipc.ts:29) stores PKCE code_verifier and state for in-flight OAuth flows. There is no timeout or cleanup mechanism. If a user opens the browser but never completes the OAuth flow, the entry leaks for the lifetime of the main process.
best_practice_anchor: Resource cleanup for in-flight async operations
recommended_slice: Add a TTL-based cleanup to pendingOAuth: expire entries after 5 minutes using setTimeout or a periodic sweep, and return a meaningful error to the renderer if the flow expires.

why_matters: ALLOWED_GITHUB_HOSTNAMES only covers api.github.com and uploads.github.com. Enterprise GitHub users with self-hosted instances (github.mycompany.com) cannot use the API proxy. This blocks an entire market segment.
best_practice_anchor: Enterprise support scope
recommended_slice: Add enterprise GitHub hostname configuration: either via electron-store settings (user-configurable) or by parsing the remote URL from the current project's git config.

why_matters: Dead export — unreferenced component adds 155 lines of maintenance surface with zero runtime reachability. Future refactors may assume it has consumers and break silently.
best_practice_anchor: Dead code removal — unreferenced module-level exports increase cognitive load and testing surface without providing value.
recommended_slice: Audit all desktop renderer dialog components for dead exports — DialogInstallPlugin is one case. Either wire it into a consumer or delete it.

why_matters: 7+ additional fire-and-forget promise patterns (dynamic imports, IPC reads, SDK calls) use `.then()` without `.catch()`. If dynamic imports fail or IPC rejects, these produce unhandled promise rejections with no error handling, telemetry, or user feedback.
best_practice_anchor: Avoid fire-and-forget Promise chains — always add .catch() or use await in try/catch
recommended_slice: Next IPC reliability lane: scan all .then() chains without .catch() across packages/app and packages/desktop, add structured error handling for dynamic import failures and SDK call rejections

why_matters: All 6 GitHub IPC handlers (github-oauth-callback, github-get-token, github-set-token, github-clear-token, github-api-proxy, github-oauth-start) except github-oauth-callback (which has PKCE validation) lack origin validation. A compromised or unexpected renderer frame could read tokens, clear tokens, or proxy arbitrary API calls.
best_practice_anchor: Electron IPC security best practices: validate sender origin on every privileged IPC handler
recommended_slice: Add origin validation middleware to all github-* IPC handlers in ipc.ts, or add a shared validateOrigin() helper that checks event.sender.getURL() against allowed origins

why_matters: The serializedWrite function stores Promises in a Map<string, Promise<void>> but returns Promise<T>, causing TS2345 error when the resolved type is not void. This blocks the typecheck from passing on the desktop package.
best_practice_anchor: TypeScript strict mode — Promise map value types must match
recommended_slice: Change writeQueues type from Map<string, Promise<void>> to Map<string, Promise<unknown>> to accommodate write functions that return values

why_matters: Two pre-existing type errors block clean typecheck in packages/desktop: (1) serializedWrite generic constraint mismatch at ipc.ts:71, (2) installCli missing on ElectronAPI type at cli.ts:7. These were not introduced by this lane but break CI type checking.
best_practice_anchor: Strict typecheck should pass for all packages before merge.
recommended_slice: Fix serializedWrite return type to align Promise<T> with internal Promise<void> chain; add installCli to ElectronAPI type or remove orphaned reference.

why_matters: renderer/cli.ts calls window.api.installCli() which was removed from ElectronAPI type — causes typecheck failure in desktop package and a runtime crash if the install CLI feature is accessed.
best_practice_anchor: Orphaned code paths must be removed or reconnected when their dependencies are deleted.
recommended_slice: Remove or reconnect the installCli function in renderer/cli.ts to match the ElectronAPI type signature.

why_matters: AgentDef and McpServerEntry/McpLocalConfig types are independently defined in both packages/app/src/types/ and packages/desktop/src/preload/types.ts with identical shapes. No shared import — creates drift risk when one side changes.
best_practice_anchor: DRY / single source of truth — types shared across package boundaries should be imported from a shared package or generated from a schema
recommended_slice: Consolidate shared types (AgentDef, McpServerEntry, McpLocalConfig) into a shared package (e.g. @opencode-ai/types) that both app and desktop can import from

why_matters: The default port 4096 appears in all i18n locale files as a placeholder string for the server URL input field. These translatable strings should ideally reference a constant, but since i18n files are flat translation dictionaries, they can't import JS constants.
best_practice_anchor: DRY principle
recommended_slice: Future slice: i18n key for server URL placeholder should use template interpolation so the port comes from a single source. This would require changing both the i18n value and the code that renders the placeholder.

why_matters: windows.ts is deleted on disk (dirty D state). The IPC refactoring plan's C8 step cannot be applied because the file doesn't exist. The ipc.ts import from ./windows will break at runtime once windows.ts is restored.
best_practice_anchor: Plan assumed windows.ts is dirty-modified (M) but it's dirty-deleted (D)
recommended_slice: After windows.ts is restored/git-checked-out, apply the two webContents.send replacements with IPC.push.* references

why_matters: 4 tool files (task.ts, edit.ts, registry.ts, write.ts) import from ./coordination.ts but the file does not exist in the tool directory. This is either a build-time alias (e.g., @/ resolves to a different location) or a dangling import that would produce a TypeScript/Node resolution error.
best_practice_anchor: All imports should resolve to existing files; using aliased imports should be consistent within the same directory.
recommended_slice: Create coordination.ts in the tool directory or switch imports to use the correct @ alias path.

why_matters: The Ambient Intelligence vision requires a macOS menu bar / Windows tray icon for background monitoring. The Electron main process currently has no tray infrastructure (no Tray import, no tray creation in main/index.ts). This is a prerequisite for the tray-based insight delivery system.
best_practice_anchor: Electron Tray API — macOS menu bar and Windows system tray app pattern
recommended_slice: Create packages/desktop/src/main/tray.ts with Tray creation, icon management, badge counter, click-to-show-popover IPC, and context menu with quick actions

why_matters: tool.ts is concurrently modified by Lane 0d (adding timeout/maxConcurrency/retry to Def interface) and by another lane (adding cacheable, R type param). The additive constraint means Lane 0d must carefully insert new fields without affecting the existing dirty state. The plan doesn't address the risk of edit conflict or the need to preserve existing changes.
best_practice_anchor: Multi-lane execution requires explicit edit conflict mitigation. See AGENTS.md Multi-Lane Execution Model section.
recommended_slice: Read the current dirty state of tool.ts, verify exact current text, and use targeted search_replace with expected_before_sha256 to insert fields additively into the Def interface without touching existing dirty regions.

why_matters: coordination.ts has been deleted from the working tree (git D) but is still imported by task.ts:15, write.ts:17, edit.ts:21, and registry.ts:42. Any build or typecheck will produce TS2307 errors for all 4 importers. This blocks any session that needs to spawn tasks, write files, or edit files — effectively breaking the entire tool pipeline.
best_practice_anchor: Never delete a module that is still actively imported by 4+ consumers without updating all import sites first. If the module was intentionally removed, its imports should have been removed in the same commit.
recommended_slice: Restore coordination.ts from git HEAD (git checkout dev -- packages/opencode/src/tool/coordination.ts) or remove all import sites and replace with the coord-events.ts mechanism if that was the intended replacement.

why_matters: Two blocker bugs (Deferred hang on compute failure, concurrent dedup race) and one major config bug (maxEntries=0) exist in the cache implementation. Under production concurrent load, compute failure will cause fiber leaks that never terminate, and concurrent requests for the same key will wastefully duplicate computation including side effects.
best_practice_anchor: Effect cachedWithTTL for dedup, Deferred.failCause in ensure/onError for failure propagation
recommended_slice: Fix cache.ts: (1) add Effect.onError to fail the Deferred on compute failure, (2) use Ref.modify for atomic inflight dedup or switch to Effect.cachedWithTTL, (3) clamp maxEntries to minimum 1, (4) add config validation for NaN/negative values

why_matters: ToolCache is used by 9+ tools (read, grep, glob, read-artifact, read-source, read-lib, rig-schema-validate, rig-jsonl-query) but has zero test coverage. Two blocker bugs (Deferred orphan on compute failure, non-atomic inflight dedup) and 4+ major bugs have no regression tests. Every tool user is exposed to untested failure paths.
best_practice_anchor: AGENTS.md: Execution wave owns minimal smoke coverage; test-engineer owns substantive tests
recommended_slice: Create packages/opencode/test/tool/cache.test.ts covering: inflight dedup atomicity, compute failure path (Deferred resolution on defect), concurrent access (fork-await pattern), expiry eviction boundary, LRU eviction at maxEntries limit, stats consistency across operations, invalidate/invalidateKey, maxEntries=0/1 edge cases, maxEntrySize bypass, negative/NaN config handling

why_matters: Duplicate lifecycle definitions exist in both definition.ts and builtins.ts with different naming conventions (CARTOGRAPHER_LIFECYCLE vs CARTographer_lifecycle). When lifecycle.ts re-exports both, name collisions and inconsistent exports will cause confusion and potentially silent export shadowing.
best_practice_anchor: Single-source-of-truth for constants — builtin lifecycle definitions should live in one file, not two
recommended_slice: Consolidate all built-in lifecycle definitions into definition.ts, remove builtins.ts, update lifecycle.ts barrel to only re-export from definition.ts and engine.ts
